summary: Documentation-only PR that added a new FAQ section to `.github/README.md` explaining the repository/codebase layout. It documented the main `src` modules, HAL two-letter folder abbreviations under `melee`, fighter abbreviations under `melee/ft/chara`, and major non-game-code areas such as `sysdolphin/baselib`, `dolphin`, `MetroTRK`, `MSL`, and `Runtime`. No review discussion or implementation code changes were present in the provided slice.
what_changed: Added an FAQ entry titled `How is the codebase structured?` to `.github/README.md`.
Explained that code in `src` is divided into modules, with `melee` being the main game code.
Documented HAL-derived two-letter `melee` folder abbreviations: `cm`, `db`, `ef`, `ft`, `gm`, `gr`, `if`, `it`, `lb`, `mn`, `mp`, `pl`, `sc`, `ty`, `un`, and `vi`.

Added notes for several modules, such as `ft` for player characters, `gm` for the main game loop, `gr` for stages/levels, `lb` for utility wrappers around `dolphin` or `baselib`, `mp` for map/stage collision code, and `sc` for game modes/scenes.
Added a `melee/ft/chara` subsection listing HAL two-letter fighter abbreviations and their fuller or canonical English names.

Documented `sysdolphin/baselib` as HAL's core internal library and listed object-class abbreviations such as `AObj`, `CObj`, `DObj`, `FObj`, `GObj`, `JObj`, `LObj`, `MObj`, `PObj`, `TObj`, `RObj`, `SObj`, and `WObj`.
Added short descriptions for `dolphin` as the Dolphin SDK, `MetroTRK` as the Metrowerks Target Resident Kernel, `MSL` as the Metrowerks Standard Library, and `Runtime` as the Gekko hardware runtime.
smart_moves: Centralized repository-layout knowledge in the README FAQ, making abbreviation conventions searchable for new contributors.
Grounded the `melee` folder abbreviations in evidence from HAL assert messages and original-disc game data, which helps avoid treating names as arbitrary project inventions.
Included both short names and fuller meanings in tables, which is useful for decompilation work where symbol, file, and directory names often preserve terse original abbreviations.

Separated canonical English fighter names from HAL/internal names where they differ, reducing ambiguity for characters such as `Pr`/Purin/Jigglypuff, `Kp`/Koopa/Bowser, `Ms`/Mars/Marth, `Sk`/Seak/Sheik, and wire-frame/Zako fighters.
Clarified that `un`/Unknown is not an actual original-code folder, which helps future contributors avoid overinterpreting that namespace.
decomp_lessons: Use existing HAL naming traces from assert messages and game data as a guide for folder/module names when available.
Maintain decomp-facing abbreviation tables because terse original names are common and can otherwise be misexpanded inconsistently.
For fighter work under `melee/ft/chara`, preserve the two-letter HAL-style prefixes and map them carefully to canonical character names only when documenting or clarifying.
Distinguish original-code organization from project-created placeholders; the README explicitly notes that `un` is not an actual folder in the original code.
Documenting subsystem boundaries helps contributors decide whether code belongs in game modules, HAL baselib, Dolphin SDK, MSL, MetroTRK, or runtime areas.
naming_conventions: Main game-code folders under `melee` use two-letter lowercase abbreviations left by HAL: `cm` Camera, `db` Debug, `ef` Effects, `ft` Fighters, `gm` Game, `gr` Ground, `if` Interface, `it` Items, `lb` Library, `mn` Menus, `mp` Map, `pl` Players, `sc` Scene, `ty` Toy, `un` Unknown, `vi` Visual.
Fighter folders under `melee/ft/chara` use two-letter capitalized abbreviations such as `Ca` Captain Falcon, `Fx` Fox, `Kb` Kirby, `Mr` Mario, `Pk` Pikachu, and `Zd` Zelda.

Some HAL/internal fighter names differ from canonical English names: `Cl` Child Link = Young Link, `Fe` Fire Emblem = Roy, `Kp` Koopa = Bowser, `Ms` Mars = Marth, `Pr` Purin = Jigglypuff, `Sk` Seak = Sheik, `Gk` Giga Koopa = Giga Bowser.
Wire-frame/Zako fighter naming was documented: `Bo` Zako Boy maps to Male wire frame and `Gl` Zako Girl maps to Female wire frame.
`Co` under fighter abbreviations denotes common/shared fighter code.

HAL baselib class abbreviations follow an `*Obj` style: `AObj` Animation, `CObj` Camera, `DObj` Draw/Display, `FObj` Frame, `GObj` Global/Game, `JObj` Joint, `LObj` Light, `MObj` Material, `PObj` Polygon, `TObj` Texture, `RObj` Reference, `SObj` Scene, `WObj` World.
follow_up_queries: Where are the current README abbreviation tables maintained after PR #1222?
Are there later PRs that refine or correct the HAL folder/fighter abbreviation mappings?
Which code files use the `melee/ft/chara` two-letter fighter prefixes documented here?
Are there naming review comments in later PRs that cite this README layout section?
